claimText: The total Priority Sector Lending (PSL) requirement for Small Finance Banks (SFBs) has been reduced from 75% to 60% of Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC), effective FY 2025-26.
evidenceExcerpt: Effective FY 2025-26, the total PSL requirement for SFBs is reduced from 75% to 60% of Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC).
examUse: Statement-based question on recent regulatory changes to Small Finance Banks.

claimText: The revised 60% PSL target for Small Finance Banks comprises a 40% mandatory allocation to PSL sectors and a 20% flexible allocation.
evidenceExcerpt: The 60% target comprises 40% mandatory allocation to PSL sectors and 20% flexible allocation.
examUse: Statement-based question on the composition of PSL targets for SFBs.
distractorHooks: Flexible allocation cannot be used for non-PSL secured loans

claimText: The Priority Sector Lending (PSL) framework applies to Commercial Banks, Regional Rural Banks (RRBs), Small Finance Banks (SFBs), Local Area Banks (LABs), and Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks (UCBs).
evidenceExcerpt: PSL framework applies to Commercial Banks, RRBs, SFBs, Local Area Banks, and Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks.
examUse: Multiple-choice question asking to identify which institutions are subject to PSL norms.
distractorHooks: Applies only to Scheduled Commercial Banks

claimText: Priority Sector Lending (PSL) categories include Agriculture, MSME, Export Credit, Education, Housing, Social Infrastructure, and Renewable Energy.
evidenceExcerpt: PSL categories include Agriculture, MSME, Export Credit, Education, Housing, Social Infrastructure, and Renewable Energy.
examUse: Identification of eligible PSL categories in a multiple-statement question.

claimText: The RBI issues Priority Sector Lending (PSL) rules for Small Finance Banks under Section 22(1) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
evidenceExcerpt: RBI issued new PSL rules for SFBs under Section 22(1) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
examUse: Statement-based question on the statutory backing of RBI's regulatory powers over SFBs.
